title: Marry Me Chicken

overview.description: Tender chicken in a creamy sauce made with sun-dried tomatoes, garlic, Parmesan and herbs.

method: Season the chicken breasts generously with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Brown the chicken breasts on both sides until golden.
Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the garlic, oregano and chilli flakes.
Cook for approximately 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken stock and scrape the bottom of the pan to release the browned bits.
Add the double cream and sun-dried tomatoes.
Return the chicken to the pan.
Simmer gently until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Scatter with fresh basil before serving.

learning.did-you-know: The dish became known as Marry Me Chicken because its rich, creamy sauce was said to be so delicious that it could inspire a marriage proposal.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a rich pan sauce by browning chicken, deglazing the pan and balancing cream, Parmesan and concentrated tomato flavour.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A rich, comforting chicken dish that introduces the children to the combination of concentrated tomato, Parmesan, garlic and herbs.
chapman-tips: Don't overcrowd the pan when browning the chicken.
Scrape the browned bits from the bottom of the pan when adding the stock.
Sun-dried tomatoes packed in oil are particularly good here, and a little of their oil can be used for frying.
Keep the sauce at a gentle simmer so the cream remains smooth.
Fresh basil should be added at the end so its flavour stays fresh.
notes.batch-cooking: Cool completely before freezing in portions. The sauce may separate slightly when reheated but should come back together with gentle heating and stirring.
notes.leftovers: Shred leftover chicken into the sauce and serve with pasta, rice or crusty bread.
